title: Apple negotiations almost complete, could iPhone 16 enter Indonesia?
body: The negotiation process between the Indonesian government and Apple is expected to be completed within the next 1-2 weeks.

title: SOE contributions to the state reach IDR 1,940 trillion
body: Non-Tax State Revenue from Separated State Assets (KND) managed by SOEs reached IDR 86.4 trillion in 2024.

title: Yan Palace increases stake in NEST to 5%
body: Yan Palace's stock rose to 5% after a purchase of 8.22 million shares on Friday (24/1).

body: After the revision, Sri Mulyani was asked to block the budgets of ministries and institutions that would be cut.

title: BI Governor: Global finance still shadowed with volatility
body: BI is committed to boosting economic growth to create investor optimism to support capital flows and economic stability.

title: Standard Chartered lowers Indonesia’s GDP growth projection to 5%
body: Reduction on Indonesia's GDP growth projection this year is in line with global economic trend that is estimated to slow down compared to 2024.

title: BNI credit grew 11.6% in FY 2024, profit edged up 2.7%
body: However, interest expenses swelled quite badly (+29.25% yoy), causing net interest income to drop to IDR 40.5 trillion.

title: Suspension lifted, DATA saw imminent drop
body: DATA saw slippage in price on its first day post-suspension.

title: IDX suspends LINK’s shares
body: IDX suspends LINK's session following significant increase in cumulative prices.

title: Mandatory one-year parking of export earnings to boost rupiah prospect
body: Increased portion of export earnings parked longer domestically is believed to be able to strengthen rupiah against US dollar.

title: PANI and AMMN dragged down JCI, corrected 0.34% to 7,232.64
body: Property sector dragged JCI to quite a severe dip of 2.95%, the worst among other sectoral indices.

title: Foreign buy reaches 3.5 billion shares; net buy 394 million shares
body: GOTO also records net buy of 545 million shares

title: Raising stakes in CAMP, Sabana Prawirawidjaja spent IDR 541.42 billion
body: Sabana Prawirawidjaja raises his stakes in CAMP, spending IDR 541.42 billion of investment.

title: CMNT secures loan facility from Bangkok Bank and Bank Permata
body: This transaction has been approved by the company’s shareholders based on the resolution of the EGMS held on December 4, 2024.

title: RONY eyes large infrastructure projects to boost growth
body: RONY targets revenue growth by expanding its construction management and architecture services market to large tender projects.

title: PGEO listed in Top 50 Global ESG 2025
body: From a governance perspective, PGEO is also targeting that by 2026, 50% of vendors involved in the procurement of goods and services will have ESG certification.

title: HRTA secures IDR 400 billion loan
body: The facility will be used for HRTA's capex and working capital.

title: Jhon Veter Firdaus now owns 5% of Brigit Biofarmaka (OBAT) shares
body: In addition, three other private investors are busy adding shares

title: HOKI readies cassava rice product
body: As of the third quarter of 2024, HOKI recorded sales growth of IDR 1.1 trillion, an increase of 19.9% year-on-year compared to IDR 888 billion in the previous year.

title: Preparing stock split, HILL sets ratio to 1:5
body: HILL is planning a stock split with 1:5 ratio, which is expected to boost its shares liquidity in the market.

title: Indonesia mandates 100% placement of export earnings, know the benefits
body: The policy will require exporters to place 100% of their forex gains from natural resource export within the country, for at least 1 year.

title: Acquiring patent for carbon absorber, OBAT optimistic for revenue growth
body: OBAT is optimistic about revenue growth after acquiring patent for carbon absorber equipment.

title: BREN’s subsidiary pairs up with SLB; what’s the goal?
body: “In order to develop the technology for geothermal industry, a collaboration with user is vital to allow us to focus on one situation that carries the most significant economic impact.”

title: Part of IDX30 now, two telco issuers show uptrends
body: Listed as IDX30 constituents, EXCL and ISAT saw strengthening trend this morning.

title: DNET secures financing from Bank Mandiri and BSI; what’s the allocation for?
body: This financing facility is categorised as non-revolving loan, with a maximum term of 13 years since the signing date, including its availability period.

title: ExxonMobil invests USD 15 billion for carbon capture storage in Indonesia
body: CCS project initiated by ExxonMobil is expected to lower CO2 emission by 90%.

title: BYD invests USD 1 billion for electric car manufacturing plant in Indonesia
body: BYD is in fierce competition with Japanese cars that have been dominating Indonesia for a long while.

title: Tech sector pushes JCI to 7,257, property sector under pressure
body: Stocks such as AMMN to TLKM led JCI gains, while PANI and PTRO weighed down

body: GOTO recorded the highest net sell again, this time 722 million shares.

title: Fore reveals recipe to dominate premium coffee market for almost 7 years
body: Although the growth has been quite expansive in recent years, Fore Coffee is optimistic that the coffee industry will continue to grow double digits.

title: SMF offers bonds and sukuk worth IDR 6.2 trillion
body: SMF plans to issue Shelf-Registration Bonds and Sukuk Musyarakah worth IDR 6.2 trillion next month to expand its financing.

title: RK reveals upstream-downstream expansion plans for Jabarano Coffee
body: RK outlined plans to increase the number of Jabarano outlets. He emphasized the huge potential for growth if the business scales beyond 10 branches.

title: Spent IDR 31.63 billion, Jonathan Tahir raises stakes in SRAJ
body: Jonathan Tahir raises his stakes at the managing company of Mayapada Hospitals.

title: WIKA secured new contracts worth IDR 20.66 trillion at the end of 2024
body: "All of these projects use a monthly payment scheme with upfront payments, allowing the company to operate independently while focusing on more efficient cash management."

title: Proven guilty of monopoly practice, KPPU fined Google LLC IDR 202.5 billion
body: KPPU imposes IDR 202.5 billion fine on Google LLC after proven guilty of monopolistic business practices.
